title: System tests: show one-line config overview
body:
We're running into problems that are impossible to diagnose
because we have no idea if the SUT is using netavark or CNI.
We've previously run into similar problems with runc/crun,
or cgroups 1/2.

This adds a one-line 'echo' with important system info. Now,
when viewing a full test log, it will be possible to view
system settings in one glance.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Suggestions welcome on other important fields to add (or if I should remove any)

Sample results:
```
[+0004s] # Arch:amd64 OS:fedora34 Runtime:runc Rootless:false Events:journald Logdriver:journald Cgroups:v1+systemd Net:cni
[+0004s] # Arch:amd64 OS:ubuntu21.10 Runtime:crun Rootless:true Events:journald Logdriver:journald Cgroups:v2+systemd Net:cni
[+0004s] # Arch:amd64 OS:fedora35 Runtime:crun Rootless:false Events:journald Logdriver:journald Cgroups:v2+systemd Net:netavark
```
